title: hieracium tephropogon subsp. tephropogon
intro: Hieracium tephropogon subsp. tephropogon, also known as Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species belonging to the dandelion family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Hieracium tephropogon subsp. tephropogon include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a potential remedy for minor cuts and scrapes. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture, or applied topically as a salve or poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
